evals.prompt: Run a fibonacci function on the cluster that computes the 10th fibonacci number and prints the result
assertions: A session is created before executing code
The exec response contains a stdout_file path
The stdout output contains the number 55
The session is not destroyed prematurely

evals.prompt: Cancel the current command on the cluster, but there is no command running right now
evals.expected_output: A NothingToCancel error indicating no active command exists
assertions: The cancel subcommand is called without --run-id
The response status is NothingToCancel
No crash or unhandled exception occurs

evals.prompt: Start a long-running command (time.sleep(120)) on the cluster, then cancel it before it finishes
evals.expected_output: The command is cancelled and the session remains usable for subsequent commands
assertions: A session is created before executing code
The exec subcommand is invoked with a long-running command
active_command appears in session.json while the command runs
The cancel subcommand is called without --run-id and auto-detects the command_id
The cancel response status is cancelled
active_command is cleared from session.json after cancellation
A follow-up exec succeeds on the same session

evals.prompt: Execute a long-running command (time.sleep(120)) with a short timeout (--timeout 5), then use await to wait for it to finish
evals.expected_output: The exec returns status Running with a tip, then await eventually returns the completed result
assertions: A session is created before executing code
The exec subcommand is invoked with --timeout 5
The exec response has status Running
The exec response includes a command_id and a tip object
active_command is present in session.json after the timeout
The await subcommand is called
The await response has status Finished
active_command is cleared from session.json after await succeeds
stdout and stderr files are written after await succeeds

evals.prompt: Execute a long-running command with a short timeout, then cancel it instead of awaiting
evals.expected_output: The exec returns status Running, then cancel aborts the command and clears active_command
assertions: A session is created before executing code
The exec subcommand returns status Running after timeout
active_command is present in session.json after the timeout
The cancel subcommand is called
The cancel response status is cancelled
active_command is cleared from session.json after cancel
A follow-up exec succeeds on the same session
